sections.paragraph:
ἐφόδιον, ου, τό (s. ὁδός) travel allowance, provisions for a journey (used Aristoph., Hdt. et al. in sg. and pl.; ins, pap; Dt 15:14; Jos., Vi. 224 al.) fig. of the provisions which Christ gives his followers for the journey of life 1 Cl 2:1 (for the fig. mng. cp. Menand., Fgm. 407, 2 Kö. ἐ. βίῳ; Epict. 3, 21, 9 ἔχοντάς τι ἐ. τοιοῦτον εἰς τὸν βίον; Plut., Mor. 160b; Sextus 551; Philo, Rer. Div. Her. 273; Jos., Bell. 1, 463; 6, 80).—DELG s.v. ὁδό.

Aristoph Aristoph , V–IV b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

  1. sg. = singular
  2. pl. = plural

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

mng. mng. = meaning(s)

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Menand Menand , various dramas cited; IV–III b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Sextus Sextus , end of II a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 419
